column1: # fqxv benchmark datasets — short-read Illumina, spanning both quality regimes.
# Fetched with `sracha` (see fetch.sh). Columns:
#   accession  label  platform  layout  quality  approx_gz  reference
# `reference` is a genome FASTA URL used only by concordance.sh (the downstream
# variant-call fidelity check); `-` = no reference (skipped). Only DNA
# resequencing datasets get one — RNA-seq needs splice-aware calling (out of
# scope), so those stay `-`.
# ERR10213669 dropped: reference-compressed cSRA, not reconstructable by sracha.
# Add a plain (unaligned) human NovaSeq run here if a binned-human sample is wanted.
# Long-read (Oxford Nanopore) — single-end, wide quality range (Phred ~0-90),
# ~14 kb mean read length. sracha writes this as DRR205413.fastq (no _1 suffix);
# run_bench.sh falls back to that name for single-end layouts.
# Long-read (PacBio HiFi) — E. coli E2348/69, same DNA as the Wick 2021
# comparison (BioProject PRJNA602597, run SRR11434954, HiFi/CCS). The full run
# is ~23 Gbp (~5000x); longread_hifi.sbatch subsamples it to ~120k reads
# (~1.5 Gbp, still ~300x) into $DATA_DIR/ecoli_hifi.fastq. Narrow high-Q, low
# error — the regime where the cross-read DNA lever matters most.
ecoli_hifi
# --- Promoted from the robustness corpus (bench/scripts/corpus.sh) -----------
# The corpus runs 48 real ENA accessions across 4 platforms but only ever
# recorded PASS/FAIL, so its ratio data went unused. These four close the
# coverage gaps the curated panel above cannot see. They are already staged in
# $CORPUS_DIR/data; fetch.sh will re-fetch them into $DATA_DIR on demand.
# PacBio Revio. ecoli_hifi above is Sequel II with a 93-symbol full-range
# quality alphabet, and ~640MB of its 656MB archive is quality — so the
# "fqxv beats CoLoRd on HiFi" result is a statement about full-range Sequel II
# quality coding. Every Revio run in the corpus has a 7-symbol alphabet
# (Phred 3-40), which collapses the quality stream and leaves sequence
# dominant. Revio is the current PacBio instrument; without these rows the
# HiFi claim is untested on the data the field actually produces.
# MGI/BGISEQ — absent from the panel entirely despite 12 corpus runs. DRR732136
# is the corpus's hardest short-read case at 5.32 bits/base (137bp, 36-symbol
# quality), well past the worst Illumina run; nothing currently measures
# whether the field handles it better.
# Ultra-short reads (22bp mean) — names dominate the archive and the sequence
# context model has almost no room to work. The panel has nothing under 100bp.
